las-vegas.title: Las Vegas
las-vegas.tagline: The Entertainment Capital of the World
las-vegas.gettingThere: Fly into Harry Reid International Airport (LAS) — major hub with direct flights from most US and many international cities. Located just 5 miles south of the Strip.
highlights: Gordon Ramsay and celebrity chef restaurants

essentials.gettingAround: Las Vegas Monorail runs along the east side of the Strip. RTC buses and the Deuce double-decker run the Strip and downtown. Rideshare widely available. Walking the Strip is common but distances are deceptive.

reno.title: Reno
reno.tagline: The Biggest Little City in the World
reno.gettingThere: Fly into Reno-Tahoe International Airport (RNO) — direct flights from major West Coast and Southwest cities. Drive from Sacramento (2.5 hours via I-80) or Las Vegas (7 hours via US-95).

essentials.gettingAround: RTC RIDE buses cover the metro area. Walkable downtown. Rental car recommended for trips to Tahoe, Virginia City, and surrounding areas.

lake-tahoe.title: Lake Tahoe
lake-tahoe.tagline: America's Most Beautiful Alpine Lake — Crystal Blue at 6,225 Feet
lake-tahoe.gettingThere: Drive from Reno (45 minutes via I-580 and NV-431 Mt. Rose Highway). Fly into Reno-Tahoe International Airport (RNO). Summer traffic on weekends can add significant time.

essentials.gettingAround: Personal vehicle or rental car essential. TART (Tahoe Area Regional Transit) runs limited routes on the north shore. Winter requires chains or snow tires.

valley-of-fire.title: Valley of Fire
valley-of-fire.tagline: Nevada's Oldest and Largest State Park — 40,000 Acres of Red Sandstone
valley-of-fire.gettingThere: Drive from Las Vegas (50 minutes northeast via I-15 and Valley of Fire Highway). No public transportation to the park.

essentials.gettingAround: Personal vehicle required. One main road (Mouse's Tank Road / Valley of Fire Highway) runs through the park with short spur roads to trailheads. $10/vehicle entrance fee.

red-rock-canyon.title: Red Rock Canyon
red-rock-canyon.tagline: A Stunning Red Rock Escape Just Minutes from the Strip
red-rock-canyon.gettingThere: Drive from the Las Vegas Strip (30 minutes west via W Charleston Blvd / NV-159). No public transit to the park.

essentials.gettingAround: Personal vehicle required. One-way 13-mile Scenic Drive loop with pullouts and trailheads. Timed entry reservation required ($2 + $15/vehicle). Arrives early on weekends.

great-basin.title: Great Basin
great-basin.tagline: Ancient Bristlecone Pines and Underground Wonders — Nevada's Only National Park
great-basin.gettingThere: Drive from Las Vegas (4.5 hours north via US-93 and US-50) or Ely (1 hour west via US-50 and NV-488). Remote location — fill up on gas in Ely or Baker.

essentials.gettingAround: Personal vehicle required. Wheeler Peak Scenic Drive (12 miles, paved) is the main road — open late May through October depending on snow. Lehman Caves tours must be reserved in advance.
essentials.airport: Ely Airport (ELY) — limited service. Harry Reid International (LAS) — 4.5 hours

virginia-city.title: Virginia City
virginia-city.tagline: Queen of the Comstock — Nevada's Legendary Silver Mining Boomtown
virginia-city.gettingThere: Drive from Reno (35 minutes southeast via NV-341) or Carson City (25 minutes northeast via US-50 and NV-341). Winding mountain road — scenic but curvy.
highlights: St. Mary's in the Mountains church

essentials.gettingAround: Walk the main C Street boardwalk on foot. Personal vehicle needed to arrive. Limited parking on busy weekends — arrive early.

henderson.title: Henderson
henderson.tagline: Nevada's Second-Largest City — Green Spaces and Desert Trails
henderson.gettingThere: Adjacent to Las Vegas — 16 miles southeast of the Strip via I-215 or I-515. 20 minutes from Harry Reid International Airport.
highlights: Ethel M Chocolate Factory and Cactus Garden

essentials.gettingAround: Personal vehicle or rideshare. RTC buses connect Henderson to Las Vegas. Walkable areas include Water Street and Lake Las Vegas Village.

boulder-city.title: Boulder City
boulder-city.tagline: Historic Dam Town — Gateway to Hoover Dam and Lake Mead
boulder-city.gettingThere: Drive from Las Vegas (30 minutes southeast via US-93/US-95). The only city in Nevada where gambling is prohibited.

essentials.gettingAround: Compact downtown is walkable. Personal vehicle needed for Hoover Dam and Lake Mead. Parking at Hoover Dam is $10.

carson-city.title: Carson City
carson-city.tagline: Nevada's State Capital — History, Hot Springs, and Mountain Views
carson-city.gettingThere: Drive from Reno (30 minutes south via US-395) or Lake Tahoe (30 minutes east via US-50). Small city at the base of the Sierra Nevada.

essentials.gettingAround: Compact downtown walkable via Kit Carson Trail. JAC bus system covers the city. Rental car needed for surrounding areas and day trips.

las-vegas-valley-of-fire.operator: I-15 North to Valley of Fire Hwy

henderson-valley-of-fire.operator: I-15 North to Valley of Fire Hwy

travelTips.currency: US Dollar (USD). Credit cards accepted everywhere in Las Vegas and Reno. Cash useful for tipping, small-town businesses, and slot machines.
travelTips.visaFree: US visa policies apply. ESTA for Visa Waiver Program countries. Valid passport required for international visitors.
travelTips.bestOverallMonths: March to May and September to November for Las Vegas and southern Nevada (pleasant desert temps). June to September for Lake Tahoe and northern Nevada (warm summers). December to March for ski season at Tahoe resorts.
travelTips.heatWarning: Summer temperatures in Las Vegas regularly exceed 110 F (43 C) June through September. Carry extra water on all desert hikes. Heat-related illness is a serious risk in Valley of Fire and Red Rock Canyon.
travelTips.hydration: Carry at least 1 liter of water per hour of hiking in desert conditions. More in summer. Start desert hikes early morning. The dry desert air dehydrates faster than you expect.
travelTips.altitude: Lake Tahoe sits at 6,225 ft elevation. Great Basin's Wheeler Peak reaches 13,063 ft. Acclimatize before strenuous hikes at altitude. Drink extra water and watch for symptoms of altitude sickness.
travelTips.parkPasses: America the Beautiful Pass ($80/year) covers Great Basin National Park and Lake Mead NRA entrance fees. Nevada state parks charge $10/vehicle. Red Rock Canyon requires timed entry reservations.
travelTips.gambling: Nevada is famous for casino gaming. Set a budget before you play. Minimum gambling age is 21. Free drinks are often served to active players on the casino floor. Tipping dealers and servers is customary.
